description: Unauthorized access to restricted files by manipulating file path parameters.

complexity_factors.question: Did the traversal successfully reach a sensitive file such as /etc/passwd or a credentials file?
complexity_factors.reason: Successful access to sensitive files means confidential data was exposed. The blue team must assess what was read, not just block the path.
complexity_factors.source: MITRE T1083 — File and Directory Discovery: access to credential files escalates impact to data exposure requiring broader investigation

complexity_factors.question: Did the attacker use encoding (e.g. %2e%2e) to bypass path normalization checks?
complexity_factors.reason: Encoded traversal sequences reduce the effectiveness of simple pattern-matching detection, requiring deeper log analysis.
complexity_factors.source: OWASP A01:2021 — Access control bypass via encoding reduces detectability

complexity_factors.question: Were application credentials or secrets exposed through the traversal?
complexity_factors.reason: Credential exposure means the blue team must assume those credentials are compromised and rotate them as part of containment.
complexity_factors.source: MITRE T1552 — Unsecured Credentials: credential exposure requires additional containment steps beyond path blocking

complexity_factors.question: Was the traversal used to gather information for a follow-on attack?
complexity_factors.reason: If traversal is used for reconnaissance, containment of traversal alone is insufficient — the blue team must monitor for follow-on exploitation.
complexity_factors.source: MITRE T1083 — often used as a precursor technique before exploitation, increasing response complexity

ttc_ranges.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: Low severity incidents should be contained rapidly with minimal escalation
NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: Medium severity incidents require analysis before containment
NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: High severity incidents involve multiple IR phases before containment is possible

rules.description: Alert must be investigated within 10 minutes of being raised
rules.trigger: No alert_investigation_started event within 600 seconds of alert_raised
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.2.4: Initial analysis should begin promptly after detection

rules.description: Incident must be confirmed — unauthorized file access must be escalated
rules.trigger: No incident_confirmed event in event log
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.2: Incident declaration is a required step in the IR lifecycle

rules.description: Containment must be initiated before max_tolerance threshold
rules.trigger: No containment_initiated event before max_tolerance_min seconds
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: Containment strategy must be selected and implemented promptly

rules.description: Containment must succeed — path blocked and file access prevented
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 — Section 3.3: Containment must be confirmed before incident can be closed

scenarios.label: Directory traversal — good response
scenarios.description: Blue team detects traversal attempt, confirms unauthorized file access, and blocks the path within acceptable time.

logs.detail: Wazuh rule 31140 fired — path traversal sequence detected in request parameter
Analyst-1 opened alert and reviewed web server access logs
Analyst-1 confirmed traversal attempt in logs, escalated
Analyst-2 applied WAF rule to block traversal sequences
Path traversal blocked, file access prevented

scenarios.label: Traversal with credential exposure — delayed response
scenarios.description: Blue team detects alert late and containment exceeds tolerance. Credentials exposed before blocking. Needs review verdict.

logs.detail: Wazuh rule 31141 fired — /etc/passwd access attempt detected
Analyst-1 confirmed credential file was accessed
Analyst-2 initiated path blocking — exceeded tolerance window
Path blocked but credentials already exposed
